english: an old legend, ancient history Zam., sṅón-gyi rabs (sngon gyi rabs) bċo-brgyad (bco brgyad) the 18 Puranas Tar. 4, 11. — 2. generation Dzl., resp. gduṅ-rábs (gdung rabs) Glr., ṅá-nas gduṅ-rábs lṅá-pa-na (nga nas gdung rabs lnga pa na) in the fifth generation after me; with respect to individuals, period of life, viz. one of the many periods, which every person is supposed to pass through, or sometimes pleon. denoting a person as being the representative of his generation: saṅs-rgyás rabs bdun (sangs rgyas rabs bdun) the seven Buddhas. — 3. in gen.; succession, series, development, e. g. the propagation of the Buddhist doctrine Tar. 205, 21; bskal-rábs (bskal rabs) successions of Kalpas, bskal-rábs-nas bskal-rábs-su (bskal rabs nas bskal rabs su).
1. W. quick-(quitch-)grass. — 2. = rán-pa? (ran pa?) Lt., Glr.

1. prob. only in: rám-bu ˳degs-pa (ram bu 'degs pa) to join in singing, to take part in a song, to fall in with, Dzl. 2V, 13 (not: to set up a dismal cry Sch.), v. also ˳čol-ba ('chol ba). — 2. = na-rám (na ram) Polygonum viviparum.
1. indigo B., col. — 2. Cs.: 'degree of doctorship, sṅags- (sngags-) or go- (go-) or druṅ-ráms-pa (drung rams pa) one having such a degree'.

1. goat's hair. — 2. rent, cleft, ṕu ral gnyis (phu ral gnyis) a sloping valley dividing into two parts at its upper end; ral-gsum (ral gsum) n. of Lahoul on account of its consisting of three valleys; cf. rál-ba (ral ba). — 3. v. rál-pa (ral pa).
1. Sch.: cleft, chink, fissure. — 2. dar-dkár-gyi rál-gu (dar dkar gyi ral gu) and rál-ka (ral ka) Pth.?

col. *ral-gyi* (ral gyi), *ra-gyi* (ra gyi) sword, also for rapier and other thrust-blades Dzl.; ral-gríi ˳dáb-ma (ral gri'i 'dab ma) or lċe (lce) blade, so (so) edge, śubs (shubs) scabbard of a sword Cs.; rál-gri-pa (ral gri pa) Cs. a sword-man; a fighting man; rál-ḱa (ral kha) = rál so (ral so); rál-ḱa spród-pa (ral kha sprod pa) 'to bring the blades together', to fight hand to hand, (ral-ḱa sbrad-pa (ral kha sbrad pa) Sch. is prob. a misprint).

long hair, lock, curl; mane (of the lion, not of the horse etc.); rál-pa-ċan (ral pa can) having or wearing long hair, n. of a Tibetan king that distinguished himself by his bigotry and by his servility to the priests; ral - lċaṅ (ral lcang) a willow planted at the birth of a child, under which a lock of the child's hair is buried, when it is seven years old Ld.

= drál-ba (dral ba) and hrál-ba (hral ba), pf. of ˳drál-ba ('dral ba), torn, of clothes etc., mtsón-gyis (mtshon gyis) lacerated, slashed, cut to pieces by the sword Dzl.; ẓig-rál-ba (zhig ral ba) id.; ẓig-rál (zhig ral) breach, destruction, ḱaṅ-pa-la ẓig-rál byúṅ-na (khang pa la zhig ral byung na) when the house gives way Glr.; ḱa-rál (kha ral), rna-rál (rna ral), sna-rál (sna ral) a lip, ear or nose, that has been lacerated by wearing rings etc.

1. sbst. cotton cloth, cottons, also a piece of cotton cloth, handkerchief etc., ras sbóm-pa (ras sbom pa) thick, strong cotton cloth; lág-ras (lag ras), ṕyis-ras (phyis ras) Cs. handkerchief, napkin; tód-ras (thod ras) turban Cs.; ṕrá-ras (phra ras) a fine sort of cotton stuff, = ḱa-śi-kai ras (kha shi ka'i ras). Comp. ras-rkyáṅ (ras rkyang) cotton cloth. — ras-skúd (ras skud) Cs. cotton thread. — ras-kúg (ras khug) a small bag made of cotton. — ras-ḱra (ras khra) calico, chintz Cs. ras-gós (ras gos) cotton dress, gown. — re-˳gá (re 'ga') a strong cotton fabric brought from Sik., C. as bċos - bu (bcos bu) Cs. calico, chintz. — ras-t́ág (ras thag) fillet, bandage. — rás-pa (ras pa) a person wearing cotton clothes Mil., frq. — ras-bál (ras bal) raw cotton. — ras - búbs (ras bubs) a whole piece of cotton cloth. — rás-ma (ras ma) a small piece, a rag Lex. *re-zén* (re zen) C. a long, loose cotton garment, shawl. — ras-yúg (ras yug) = ras-búbs (ras bubs). — ras-rú (ras ru) v. re-rú (re ru). — ras-slág (ras slag) a furred garment covered with cotton cloth W. — 2. adj. ḱa-rás (kha ras) (*rë* (re), for reṅs? (rengs?)) hard snow that will bear a man.

1. vb., Ld. *ras-ċe* (ras ce) to get or grow hoarse, *skad ras soṅ* (skad ras song) the voice has grown hoarse, *skad ras-sa rag* (skad ras sa rag) I feel a hoarseness in my throat: — 2. sbst. v. sub ras (ras).

also ri-bo (ri bo) B., *ri-ga* (ri ga) W. 1. mountain, hill, ri pó-ta-la (ri po ta la) the mountain (called) Potala Liś.; ri-bo dpal-˳bár (ri bo dpal 'bar) Mil., rgyal-gyi-sri ri (rgyal gyi sri ri) Mil. the mountain Pal-bár, Gyal-gyisrí; rir (rir) on the mountain Mil., ri-la (ri la) id.
